title: Centre for Cybersecurity - Home
gist: A World Economic Forum institutional landing page describing its Centre for Cybersecurity's mission, scale of partners, and three ongoing initiatives (AI & Cybersecurity, Partnership against Cybercrime, Cyber Resilience) — no news content, dates, or author; purely a mission/marketing overview.

insights.claim: The World Economic Forum's Centre for Cybersecurity coordinates 163 business partners and 52 public-sector partners across 3 ongoing initiatives, positioning itself as an independent public-private platform for global cybersecurity coordination.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: A scale marker for how large the WEF's cybersecurity coalition has grown — useful context for tracking multilateral cyber-policy coordination bodies.

insights.claim: One of the Centre for Cybersecurity's three active initiatives is specifically focused on AI & Cybersecurity, developing guidance to help leaders adopt AI technologies securely.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Signals that AI-security guardrails are being formalized at the multilateral institutional level, which may precede or inform future industry/regulatory standards.
insights.quote: AI & CybersecurityBringing together a global multi-stakeholder community to develop actionable guidance to help leaders adopt AI technologies in a secure manner.

insights.claim: The Centre runs a dedicated initiative on strengthening cybersecurity in the oil and gas industry, involving over 40 private and public organizations.
insights.speaker: World Economic Forum
insights.so_what: Highlights energy-sector critical infrastructure as a specific WEF cybersecurity focus area, relevant to energy-sector cyber-risk monitoring.
insights.quote: Strengthening cybersecurity in the oil and gas industryA community of over 40 private and public organizations work together to drive forward collective action in cyber resilience.
insights.locator: "Our impact" section, oil and gas item
